sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 6.4 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.6)

statement.latex: A group$G$is called of type$(FP)_{$} if the trivial$G$-module${ Z}$has a resolution by finitely generated projective$G$-modules. Is it true that every torsion-free group of type$(FP)_{ }$has finite cohomological dimension? { R.\,Bieri} No (K.\,S.\,Brown, R.\,Geoghegan, Invent. Math., { 77} (1984), 367--381). { {Archive of solved problems (6th ed., 1978)}}{ {Archive of solved problems (6th ed., 197...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
